old_claim.note: Printing Press provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No printing dependency remains because the node now covers enrolled detailed specifications; Patent Office publication of historical grants was a later archival program.

source_shape.source_locator: Sections 2 and 4 distinguish original enrolled specifications from copies printed and published after the Patent Office assumed responsibility in 1852.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: British technical specifications were enrolled legal records before the later Patent Office retrospective publication series.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Conflating archival printing with the original disclosure assigns a nineteenth-century reproduction method to a 1711 record.

ontology_before: The 1711 disclosure was described as contemporaneously printed through a generic Gutenberg citation.
ontology_after: The node represents detailed patent specifications; retrospective Patent Office printing remains outside its chronology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_patent_specifications no longer directly depends on printing_press.
would_reject_if: A primary record established that the scoped 1711 detailed specification was issued through contemporaneous print publication.
short_reason: Later archival printing is not the original specification process.
